平成年から西暦へ即座に変換 | Convert Heisei era dates instantly

Convert any Heisei (平成) era year to the Western Gregorian calendar. The Heisei era spans from 1989 to 2019, covering 31 years of Japanese history under Emperor Akihito. Enter a Heisei year below for an instant conversion.

Heisei to Western Year — Quick Reference Table

Heisei Year (平成)Western YearNotes
平成1年 (元年)1989Era began Jan 8
平成5年1993
平成10年1998
平成15年2003
平成20年2008
平成25年2013
平成30年2018
平成31年2019Only Jan 1–Apr 30; then Reiwa

About the Heisei Era (平成時代)

The Heisei era (平成, meaning "achieving peace") lasted 31 years from January 8, 1989 to April 30, 2019, under the reign of Emperor Akihito. The era began during the peak of Japan's economic bubble, which burst spectacularly in the early 1990s, leading to what economists call the "Lost Decade" of economic stagnation. Despite economic challenges, the Heisei period saw Japan emerge as a global leader in technology, with the rise of the internet, mobile phones, and Japanese pop culture's worldwide expansion.

The Heisei era was also shaped by significant natural disasters, including the Great Hanshin Earthquake of 1995 and the devastating 2011 Tohoku earthquake and tsunami. The era ended uniquely in Japanese history when Emperor Akihito abdicated on April 30, 2019, the first abdication in over 200 years. His son, Emperor Naruhito, ascended the throne on May 1, 2019, beginning the Reiwa era. To convert any Heisei year to the Western calendar, use the formula: Heisei year + 1988 = Western year. For example, Heisei 15 equals 2003, and Heisei 30 equals 2018.

Frequently Asked Questions

How do I convert Heisei to Western year?

To convert a Heisei year to the Western (Gregorian) calendar, simply add 1988 to the Heisei year number. For example, Heisei 20 equals 1988 + 20 = 2008. This formula works for every year of the Heisei era, from Heisei 1 (1989) through Heisei 31 (2019). Use the converter tool above for instant results with optional month and day precision.

When did the Heisei era end?

The Heisei era ended on April 30, 2019, when Emperor Akihito formally abdicated the Chrysanthemum Throne. This was the first abdication by a Japanese emperor in over 200 years, making it a historic event in Japan's imperial history. The following day, May 1, 2019, marked the beginning of the Reiwa era under Emperor Naruhito, his eldest son.

What is Heisei 31 in Western calendar?

Heisei 31 corresponds to the year 2019 in the Western calendar. However, Heisei 31 covers only the first four months of 2019, from January 1 to April 30. On May 1, 2019, the Reiwa era began following Emperor Akihito's abdication. So while 1988 + 31 = 2019 is correct, any date after April 30, 2019 should be expressed as Reiwa 1 rather than Heisei 31.
